mnemonics: TREES: Trial and Error to Rule Educational Success
Binary Trees: Brought In By Experts Everyday Simplifying
keyPoints: Introduction to Trees: Root, Trunk, Branch, and Leaves
Definition: A tree is a non-linear data structure, where each node has a value and zero or more child nodes
Properties: Balanced, Binary Search, and Connected
Types of Trees: Deciduous, Evergreen, and Sycamore
Binary Trees: A tree where each node has at most two child nodes (left and right)
Binary Tree Traversals: Inorder, Preorder, and Postorder
Threaded Binary Trees: A binary tree where each node has a thread or reference to its left child
